body: {"rationale":"Investigation bead with clear problem statement, evidence (specific bead IDs, costs, route reasons), and required gates. Weak spots: root cause cites cli/cmd/work.go but no specific line and explicitly says 'needs confirmation' (investigation bead, acceptable but reduces specificity); no explicit parent/deps declaration; AC #1/#2 allow flexible deliverable forms ('test, diagnostic fixture, or captured execution record') which softens the wired-in assertion. Title, NON-SCOPE, labels, spec-id, and gate ACs (#5 with -run filter, #6 lefthook) are all present and standalone.","score":6,"suggested_fixes":["Add explicit 'Parent: none' and 'Deps: none' (or list them) per rubric criterion 8.","Tighten AC #1 and #2 to name the specific artifact form (e.g., a Go test file path or fixture under cli/internal/agent/testdata) rather than 'test, diagnostic fixture, or captured execution record'.","Replace the vague cli/cmd/work.go reference with a concrete symbol/line where the routing request is constructed (e.g., the function that builds the Fizeau request envelope) so the executor has a starting edit site.","Name the upstream Fizeau policy/profile file or registry entry to inspect, so the investigation has a bounded surface."],"waivers_applied":[]}
